name: Low-Calorie Potato, Onion, and Garlic Gratin
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the papery covering of the garlic heads without peeling or separating cloves. Wrap in foil and bake at 425°F for 1 hour.
2. Place onions on a double layer of foil and bake at 425°F for 1 hour and 30 minutes, or until very soft.
3. After an hour, remove garlic heads, unwrap, let cool for 5 minutes, separate cloves, and press into a bowl to release softened garlic.
4. After 1.25 hours, remove onions. If not fully soft, return them for a few more minutes.
5. Cut off stem and root ends of onions, then peel the outer skin and first layer. Pulse in food processor until smooth.
6. Lower oven temperature to 350°F. Mix onions, garlic, potatoes, salt, and pepper.
7. Stir in buttermilk. Transfer mixture to a gratin dish, smoothing top and sprinkling with Parmesan.
8. Drizzle milk evenly over potatoes. Bake uncovered for 35-45 minutes until brown, bubbly, and hot.
9. Serve immediately.
